AI Agent Project Presentation

GitHub Dev Card Generator

An AI-powered web application that turns any public GitHub profile into a premium developer showcase card using Google ADK, FastMCP, Gemini 2.5 Flash, FastAPI, Cloud Run, and Vertex AI Memory Bank.

ServerlessFrontend and backend run on Cloud Run with scale-to-zero behavior.
AgenticGoogle ADK coordinates tool use instead of hardcoded step chaining alone.
PersonalizedMemory Bank keeps durable session context and preference recall.
ShareableEach generated card is saved and served as a linkable HTML asset.

What the project does

The app accepts a public GitHub username, gathers profile and repository data, sends the structured data to Gemini 2.5 Flash for analysis, and generates a polished HTML developer card with a custom vibe, skill badges, top repositories, and a memorable summary.

Instead of returning plain analytics, it produces a visual artifact that is easy to share in portfolios, communities, hackathons, and social profiles. This makes GitHub activity more accessible, more presentable, and more engaging for both technical and non-technical audiences.

Google ADK FastMCP Gemini 2.5 Flash FastAPI Vertex AI Memory Bank Cloud Run NGINX
Live web interface showing username input, card generation flow, and the rendered developer card.

Why it is impactful

1. Better developer storytelling

It converts raw GitHub metadata into a concise, human-readable profile that highlights strengths, interests, and project focus in seconds.

2. Real agent architecture

It demonstrates a production-style AI workflow with orchestration, tool calling, memory, API integrations, and cloud deployment.

3. Practical personalization

Memory Bank enables continuity across sessions, which is a core capability for assistants that need to adapt to user preferences over time.

Example output card generated from a public GitHub profile, combining profile statistics, inferred skills, top repositories, and a themed visual layout.

Component responsibilities

Frontend service

A clean HTML and JavaScript interface collects the GitHub username, calls the backend API, shows loading states, and renders the final card for sharing.

Backend service

The FastAPI application exposes API endpoints, manages sessions, invokes the ADK runner, and serves the saved card assets to end users.

ADK agent

The agent enforces the generate flow by coordinating tool calls in sequence: fetch data, analyze profile, generate HTML, and save the card.

MCP server

FastMCP exposes tool functions in a standard protocol, allowing the agent to interact with GitHub data retrieval, Gemini analysis, and HTML generation through a unified interface.

Vertex AI Memory Bank

Persistent memory stores session context and user preferences so the experience can become more adaptive and personalized across multiple uses.

Architecture diagram

The system separates user experience, orchestration, tooling, intelligence, and persistence into clear service boundaries. This keeps the design modular, testable, and easy to deploy on managed cloud infrastructure.

Updated architecture: User → HTTPS → Cloud Run Frontend → FastAPI Backend → Google ADK Agent (Stdio MCP) → MCP Tool Server → GitHub REST API + Google Cloud Vertex AI (Gemini 2.5 Flash). Vertex AI Memory Bank handles persistent cross-session state.

End-to-end flow

  • The user enters a GitHub username in the frontend.
  • The frontend sends a generate request to the FastAPI backend hosted on Cloud Run.
  • The backend runner invokes the ADK agent and loads the relevant session and memory context.
  • The agent calls FastMCP tools to fetch GitHub profile data and repository metadata.
  • The tool layer sends structured profile data to Gemini 2.5 Flash on Vertex AI for analysis.
  • The generated insights are turned into a self-contained HTML dev card and saved for serving.
  • The backend returns the card result so the frontend can render it inline and share it via URL.
Project value: This project shows how modern AI systems can move beyond chat and generate practical, reusable user-facing artifacts by combining agent orchestration, protocol-based tools, memory, and serverless deployment in one cohesive application.
